_doc: Per-fixture expected output ranges for the analysis pipeline.
The runner generates each fixture deterministically from synth.py,
feeds it through `_analyze_file()`, and asserts every numeric output
lands inside the band declared here. Values are PINNED to the current
production pipeline (v2.6) — so any code change that shifts a value
outside its band fails CI loudly, forcing an explicit manifest update.
Range syntax:
[lo, hi]       — closed numeric range, must be inside.
null           — value MUST be None.
'not_null'     — value MUST be populated (any non-None).
{'error': S}   — analysis_error MUST contain substring S; scalar
feature columns must be missing or null.
Bands have ~10-15% headroom around observed values to tolerate
non-determinism from ONNX provider differences (CPU vs CUDA) without
swallowing real regressions. If a fixture starts failing here on a
code change, treat it as a signal first, not noise.
Applied to every fixture except those with an expected error.
Caught historically: #42 (zero-norm embedding), #83 (NULL embedding
leak), CLAP-text BOS-padding (cos > 0.99), #98 (CLAP cosine spread).

silence._doc: 12s of pure zeros. Pins the analyser's silence behaviour.

too_short._doc: 5s — must short-circuit with the 'Track too short' error.

white_noise._doc: Broadband noise. Spectrally flat, no tonal content.

drum_loop._doc: Programmed kick+snare+hat at 120 BPM. Strong rhythm.

acoustic_arpeggio._doc: Slow plucked-string arpeggio in C major.

_doc: Synthesized voice-shaped signal: glottal pulse train + formant
filtering + syllable-rate AM. The EffNet voice classifier doesn't
fully recognise it as voice (synthesis is too clean), so this
fixture is NOT a #99 smoking gun — Layer 3 (DB invariant against
taste_profile.py priors) catches that. Kept for embedding/CLAP
sanity on a non-music-shaped signal.

distorted_metal._doc: Distorted power chord, palm-mute rhythm at 160 BPM.
